name: Jamaican Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces Semisweet chocolate, broken into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. Generously grease a 5-cup fluted or plain tube pan and dust lightly with flour.
3. In a bowl, sift together the self-rising flour, baking powder, and Mixed Spice.
4. Add margarine, superfine sugar, and eggs to the flour mixture. Blend until well combined.
5. Sift cocoa powder into hot water and mix until smooth. Add this mixture to the flour mixture.
6. Turn the batter into the prepared pan.
7. Bake in the preheated oven for 1-1/4 hours or until well risen and the cake begins to shrink from the edges of the pan.
8. Carefully turn out the cake onto a wire rack and cool completely.
9. In a saucepan, combine granulated sugar and 2/3 cup water. Add cinnamon and heat gently, stirring to dissolve the sugar. Boil for 5 minutes.
10. Remove from heat, add rum, and discard the cinnamon. Place the cake on a plate. Pour the syrup over the cake and let stand for 2 hours.
11. Sprinkle almonds evenly over the top of the cake.
12. Melt chocolate and carefully spoon it over the cake, spreading to give a smooth even coating.
13. Let stand several hours before serving.
14. Pipe (with a pastry bag) whipped cream around the bottom of the cake.
